title: GLM-5.2
description: A later GLM line member that extends sparse-attention mixture-of-experts architecture with a solid 1M-token context and long-horizon coding emphasis.
openingSummary: GLM-5.2 matters because it keeps the GLM sparse-attention, mixture-of-experts stack but pushes the line toward long-horizon coding work. The official release blog emphasizes a stable 1M-token context, IndexShare efficiency for sparse attention, and training aimed at sustained agentic engineering rather than one-shot answers. The Z.ai GitHub download table lists the checkpoint at 744 billion total parameters with about 40 billion active per token.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: GLM-5.2 is an open-weights text model from Z.ai, released on 2026-06-17 in the GLM-5.2 blog post. It is the later GLM line member that builds on the same sparse mixture-of-experts transformer family as GLM-5 while adding a 1M-token context window under an MIT open-source license. The official GitHub model download table lists it at 744 billion total parameters and about 40 billion active parameters.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: GLM-5.2 is a text-in, text-out model. The public release emphasizes long coding-agent trajectories, tool use, and multi-step engineering work where the model must keep quality across very long prompts instead of degrading once context grows.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: GLM-5.2 keeps the decoder-only sparse MoE skeleton from the GLM line. The release blog highlights IndexShare, which reuses a lightweight indexer across every four sparse-attention layers to cut indexer cost at 1M context, plus improved multi-token prediction for speculative decoding. The named building blocks still include DSA-style sparse attention, mixture-of-experts routing, multi-head latent attention, RMSNorm, and RoPE.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: These modules explain why GLM-5.2 stays in the same modern long-context MoE lane as GLM-5 rather than becoming a separate architecture family. Sparse attention and mixture-of-experts carry the scaling story, while latent attention, RMSNorm, and RoPE support stable training and very long prompts.

training.title: Training
training.body: The GLM-5.2 blog frames post-training around long-horizon agentic reinforcement learning, on-policy distillation from multiple expert models, and anti-hack safeguards for coding-agent rollouts. The public materials describe expanded 1M-context training for coding-agent scenarios and open-weight release in bf16.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: GLM-5.2 advertises a 1,048,576-token context window and effort-level control that lets callers trade latency against reasoning depth. Read GLM-5 first for the original agentic-engineering story and shorter advertised context, then use this page when you need the later line member with the 1M-context and long-horizon coding emphasis.

model.glm-5.reason: GLM-5 is the earlier GLM line member that introduced the sparse-attention MoE stack and original agentic-engineering positioning.

architectureGraph.alt: GLM-5.2 architecture diagram with token embeddings, RoPE position encoding, a repeated decoder block containing sparse attention, RMSNorm, and mixture-of-experts routing, then an output projection and softmax.

outputProbabilities.label:
Output
Probabilities
outputProbabilities.summary: The model's probability distribution over the next token

softmax.label: Softmax
softmax.summary: Turns final vocabulary scores into probabilities

linear.label:
Output
Linear
linear.summary: Projects the final hidden state into vocabulary logits

decoderStack.summary: Repeated decoder block container for the main GLM-5.2 stack

repeatMarker.label: N×
repeatMarker.summary: This decoder block repeats through the full stack

rmsnormAttention.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormAttention.summary: Normalizes the residual stream before the sparse attention step

sparseAttention.label:
Sparse
Attention
(DSA)
sparseAttention.summary: The attention path uses DSA-style sparsity with IndexShare to cut long-context cost while keeping useful global context

rmsnormMoe.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormMoe.summary: Normalizes the stream before the sparse expert block

mixture-of-experts.label:
Mixture
Of Experts
mixture-of-experts.summary: Routes each token through a small active set of experts instead of one dense feed-forward path

ropeInject.summary: Applies token states together with rotary position information before the stack

rope.label: RoPE
rope.summary: Rotary position encoding gives the attention stack token order information

inputEmbedding.label:
Input
Embedding
inputEmbedding.summary: Maps token IDs into hidden vectors
